programmes.show_description: Tracy's been single for three years; can the revealing dating show find her a man? Phone salesman Matthew is bored of dating apps and wants women to see the real him.
The Goggleboxers watch Eurovision, Citadel, The Greatest Auction, Family Fortunes, Open House: The Great Sex Experiment, I Kissed a Boy and The Big Celebrity Detox.
Brand new series: After weeks of honeymoons filled with love, laughter and tonnes of tiffs, the couples return to face their first major test: the first dinner party.
Brand new series: Inga and Jazz's ever-tightening friendship doesn't go unnoticed. And will an unexpected bombshell spell disaster for Livvy and Kit's rekindled romance?
Yokel Chords: In a parody of The Sound of Music, Lisa tutors Cletus's seven children. But when Krusty spots their singing talent, he snaps them up for his circus.
The Beverly Goldberg Cookbook: Beverly and Adam appear on a mother-and-son cookery show on public access TV. Barry ruins his college letter of recommendation.
Eight-bit Goldbergs: Adam's thrilled when he's asked to design his own video game for a school project. Beverly's worried that Pops loves gambling too much.
In 2011, Anna turned wedding planner for her girly fiancée Sian. They're back to relive the climactic events that nearly led Anna to cancel their big day.
Lucas has a tough decision to make in order to protect himself from a dangerous cellmate. Elsewhere, Warren is determined to uncover the truth from someone, no matter the cost.
While Tony and Diane head to London for answers, Dom's left holding the fort - but the teens have other plans. Ste returns to devastating news. And Warren is determined to get to the truth.
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sheep.
Blackberry's: Gordon visits New Jersey to save floundering soul food restaurant Blackberry's. Owner Shelley doesn't want to hear his advice, but then Gordon finds a dead mouse.
This Is This Is Spinal Tap: The Dropouts are booked to perform a gig at a charity carnival. Beverly's concerns about Murray's health push him to the limit.
I Lost on Jeopardy!: Erica applies to be on TV quiz show Jeopardy! A new crush makes Barry realise that he might not be quite over Lainey yet.
Finale (Part 1/2): Mitchell and Cam settle on their new normal, and Phil and Claire decide that one of the kids needs to move out so they can take control of the house again.
Finale (Part 2/2): In the last ever episode, the entire family discover that saying goodbye is much harder than it seems.
Brand new series: After weeks of honeymoons filled with love, laughter and tonnes of tiffs, the couples return to face their first major test: the first dinner party.
Beauty queen bricklayer Chloe seeks a girl with grand designs for the future. India wants a tall, dark, handsome guy. And financial investigator Sylthea gives her date a grilling.
Leone's: Rose built her Italian restaurant in New Jersey into a thriving business, but when she spent two years in hospital, her son ran it into the ground. Can Gordon save it?
G.I. (Annoyed Grunt): Homer accidentally signs up for basic training in the army. And Marge and Grampa get roped in when they try to get him released.
Moe 'n' a Lisa: Lisa discovers Moe's poetic talent and helps get him published. And he's so successful that he - and the Simpsons - are invited to a literary conference.
Fizbo: Phil and Claire throw Luke the biggest birthday party a boy could want. But when Cameron appears as a clown named Fizbo, it sets off a chain of events that ends in hospital.
Undeck the Halls: Christmas is in full swing at the Dunphy house, until the kids get into trouble and Phil and Claire threaten to cancel Christmas unless one of them fesses up.
Brand new series: After weeks of honeymoons filled with love, laughter and tonnes of tiffs, the couples return to face their first major test: the first dinner party.
In Lisbon, some loved-up newbies join the trip, while one celeb is missing, and the rivalry heats up in bubble football. Who'll be shown the red card at the vote?
Rome-Old & Juli-eh: Homer stops paying for Grampa's retirement home, so he has to stay with the family instead. In a twist no one saw coming, Grampa ends up marrying Selma.
Homerazzi: Homer becomes a paparazzo to get money to pay for fire damage to the house. But Springfield's celebrities are alarmed by his aggressive style and fight back.
A revelation leaves Warren seething, and tensions flare at The Loft. Meanwhile, when a text lands in the wrong hands, a resident is forced into a tough parenting dilemma.
...Australia - Brand new series: The newly-weds move in together and face tasks designed to make them more honest with each other. But honesty isn't always the best policy...
On the 'Box this time: Casualty, Queer Eye, Below Deck Sailing Yacht, FUBAR, Open House: The Great Sex Experiment, Big Beasts and BBC News (Phillip Schofield).
The Goggleboxers have been watching: Sitting on a Fortune, Casualty, Britain's Got Talent, MerPeople, James May: Oh Cook!, The Greatest Auction and Top of the Pops 1994.
Anna has a lengthy list of what she wants from her perfect man. Her date Liam likens dating to hunting a gazelle. Adela is looking for an archetypal English gent.
Sapphire hopes to find her ideal woman by stripping back the clothes. Kieron, who's been single for three years, chooses from six women in the Naked Attraction pods.
Brand new series: The newly-weds move in together and face tasks designed to make them more honest with each other. But honesty isn't always the best policy...
On the 'Box this time: Casualty, Queer Eye, Below Deck Sailing Yacht, FUBAR, Open House: The Great Sex Experiment, Big Beasts and BBC News (Phillip Schofield).
This Is This Is Spinal Tap: The Dropouts are booked to perform a gig at a charity carnival. Beverly's concerns about Murray's health push him to the limit.
I Lost on Jeopardy!: Erica applies to be on TV quiz show Jeopardy! A new crush makes Barry realise that he might not be quite over Lainey yet.
Becky and Lee sit down to reminisce about their a-maze-ing wedding back in 2018, when Lee challenged his bride to find him before they said their vows.
Yes-Woman: To be less negative, Claire rebrands herself as the woman who says 'yes', and inadvertently approves of Luke's new relationship with a colleague.
Can't Elope: Haley and Dylan's plan to quietly elope before the babies are born does not go as hoped. Jay tries to get his new business off the ground by courting an Insta celeb.
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister, Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorbike and bicycle variety.
Leone's: Rose built her Italian restaurant in New Jersey into a thriving business, but when she spent two years in hospital, her son ran it into the ground. Can Gordon save it?
Mom Trumps Willow: Adam decides to see the movie Willow instead of spending Mother's Day with Beverly. Barry is tired of being the middle child.
Breakin': Adam reveals that Barry never completed his community service requirement. Erica plans for her and Geoff to follow The Grateful Dead all summer.
Pilot: The first episode of the Emmy-winning comedy, shot from the perspective of an unseen documentary maker. It's not easy being part of a family these days...
The Bicycle Thief: Phil is determined to help Luke show his mum that he's responsible enough to have the new bike they just got him, but Phil ends up being the irresponsible one.
Brand new series: The newly-weds move in together and face tasks designed to make them more honest with each other. But honesty isn't always the best policy...
Ex-strongman Matt meets midwife Kat, who has a big story to tell. Will Devon housekeeper Luke's dream of an 'upbeat, fun Latino' come to life in the shape of David from Portugal?
The Burger Kitchen (Part 1/2): In the first half of a two-part Nightmare, Gordon tries to save a burger joint in LA, run by a dysfunctional family in a state of constant conflict.
Ice Cream of Margie (With the Light Blue Hair): Marge, feeling overshadowed by Homer's new career selling ice cream, starts creating ice lolly stick sculptures.
The Haw-Hawed Couple: Bart becomes Nelson's best friend when Marge makes him go to his birthday party. Which means no one can mess with Bart. But there are downsides too...
Up All Night: Gloria isn't happy with Jay's reaction to the arrival of Javier, Manny's infamously unreliable dad. Phil is suffering from kidney stones.
Brand new series: The newly-weds move in together and face tasks designed to make them more honest with each other. But honesty isn't always the best policy...
One pair feel the pressure in Lisbon. The celebs make custard tarts and enjoy a helicopter ride, but back on solid ground, plotting is rife ahead of a tense vote.
Marge Gamer: Marge gets hooked on an online fantasy game and discovers that Bart is the game's best - and most destructive - player.
The Boys of Bummer: Just as the Springfield Isotopes are on the verge of winning their first Little League Championship, Bart makes a mistake that costs the team their title.
Lisa's Belly: Bart and Lisa have to take steroids after getting an infection at a waterpark - and the new drugs have some alarming side effects, particularly for Lisa.
...Australia - Brand new series: Revelations Week escalates with a confrontational task that prompts shocking confessions, brutal opinions and a blazing argument.
Britain's sharpest armchair critics share their insightful and passionate takes on the best TV shows from series 21.
Britain's sharpest armchair critics watch Gordon, Gino & Fred: Viva España, EastEnders, Top Guns: Inside the RAF, Beat the Chasers and The Yorkshire Vet.
